title: toechima erythrocarpum subsp. papuanum
intro: Toechima erythrocarpum subsp. papuanum, also known as the Papuan toechima or red fruit toechima, is a plant species in the family Sapindaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Toechima erythrocarpum subsp. papuanum are attributed to its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its potential to treat various diseases such as malaria and dysentery. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are then used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
